entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: An important legume crop of the papilionaceae family, characterized as a hard woody shrub.

entities.name: PAPILIONACEAE FAMILY
entities.description: The botanical family to which pigeonpea belongs.

entities.name: CROSS-POLLINATION
entities.description: The process of pollination where pollen is transferred between different plants, occurring at an average of 20% in pigeonpea.

entities.name: BREEDING PRINCIPLES
entities.description: Principles of self and cross-pollinated crops used for genetic enhancement.

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The improvement of genetic traits in a crop.

entities.name: SOIL TYPES
entities.description: Various soil environments to which pigeonpea is extensively adaptable.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental temperature ranges to which pigeonpea is adaptable.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Environmental rainfall levels to which pigeonpea is adaptable.

entities.name: DEEP TAPROOT SYSTEM
entities.description: A root system extending up to two meters in depth.

entities.name: BRADY RHIZOBIUM SPP
entities.description: Bacteria that form a symbiotic association with pigeonpea roots.

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into a usable form, performed by Brady rhizobium spp.

entities.name: BRANCHING PATTERN
entities.description: The stem growth pattern, varying from bush type to compact upright type.

entities.name: FLOWERING PATTERN
entities.description: The pattern of flowering that determines the determinate, semi-determinate, or non-determinate nature of the crop.

entities.name: POD
entities.description: The fruit of the pigeonpea, appearing in various colors with or without deep constrictions.

entities.name: SEEDS
entities.description: The reproductive units of pigeonpea, containing proteins and amino acids, appearing in round or lens shapes.

entities.name: PROTEINS
entities.description: Nutritional components of pigeonpea seeds, comprising 20-22% of the seed.

entities.name: AMINO ACIDS
entities.description: Nutritional components found within pigeonpea seeds.
